{{Quote|What is the good, if not the teacher of the bad? What is the bad, if not the task of the good?|The Question of Master Jrul|<ref name="Revenge of the Sith novel" />}}
'''Jrul''' was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived sometime before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The Master was attributed with formulating the famous [[Question of Master Jrul]]. Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] remembered Jrul's Question when he encountered the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of the General's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']],<ref name="Revenge of the Sith novel" /> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Jrul was mentioned in the [[2005]] novel [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith'']] written by [[Matthew Stover]].<ref name="Revenge of the Sith novel" />

==Appearances==
